results.title: Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ:META) | Valuation Ratios
results.content: :   The P/E ratio began at 16.44 in 2021 and increased to 21.10 in 2022, reflecting the greater decline in share price relative to earnings. :   The share price experienced a decrease from US$237.76 in 2021 to US$188.77 in 2022, which likely contributed to the initial increase in the P/OP ratio, as operating profit per share also decreased. Continued increases in both share price and operating profit per share in 2024 and 2025, though at a diminishing rate for share price, resulted in a moderate decrease in the P/OP ratio in 2025. The ratio’s decline in the latter years of the period suggests that while the share price continued to increase, the growth in operating profit per share was sufficient to partially offset this increase in terms of valuation. Finally, the ratio decreased slightly to 8.60 in 2025, as the share price increase slowed while book value per share continued to grow.

The valuation data shows tech is expensive but not at bubble extremes (NVDA PEG 1.6, MSFT PEG 1.6 are reasonable for growth). TSLA at 307x P/E with negative growth is the clear outlier. results.title: Nasdaq 100 Analysis 20/03: Tests Support (Video&Chart)
results.author: Christopher Lewis
highlights:
Nasdaq 100 Analysis 20/03: Tests Support (Video&Chart)

Start Trading Now Get Started 1. DF
2. Stock Market Today
3. NASDAQ 100 Price Analysis – NASDAQ 100 Sitting on Support # NASDAQ 100 Price Analysis – NASDAQ 100 Sitting on Support

Christopher Lewis   ...    Read more

Created on March 20, 2026 - The Nasdaq 100 has been very noisy in early Thursday trading, as traders are watching the ten-year yield test the 4.30& area. - The markets are still a nervous place to be. The Nasdaq 100 has been very noisy in early trading on Thursday as we had seen yields spike in America. That of course is a negative turn of events for stocks in general, but I also recognize that the 200-day EMA sitting just below probably continues to offer a little bit of psychological support. This is a market that despite the world being on fire has been sideways for a while and I think that is a testament to its resilience and its underlying importance to the financial system. ## Support Floors and Growth Drivers The 23,800 level for me is a line in the sand and if that gets broken to the downside then I think we probably have much further to go. Alternatively, if it holds as support and we turn around and retake the 25,000 level, I'd be very interested in buying this market because I think it opens up a move back to the highs.   ...    This will move probably on geopolitics more than anything else, but it is worth noting that the numbers coming out of some companies like NVIDIA have been very impressive. So, with that, I do believe eventually we will go higher. I just think that it is going to be a bumpy, noisy ride along the way. The usual “buy on the dip” strategy, I think that remains the call here, although you're going to have to deal with a lot of noise and nausea along the way would be my guess. Again, I'm watching that 23,800 level for a floor. As long as that holds, I'm optimistic. I'm

results.title: Tech Tumble: Nasdaq Slips Into Correction as Geopolitical Storms ...
highlights: Tech Tumble: Nasdaq Slips Into Correction as Geopolitical Storms and Interest Rate Fears Converge | FinancialContent   ...    The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQ:IXIC) officially entered correction territory yesterday, March 26, 2026, following a sharp 2.4% decline that sent the index to a closing level of 21,408.08. This retreat marks a   ...    record peak in late October 2025. Investors are grappling with a "volatile cocktail" of escalating Middle Eastern tensions and a Federal Reserve that appears increasingly unwilling to lower interest rates in the face of war-induced   ...    Yesterday’s sell-off was the culmination of several weeks of building pressure, but the catalyst for the 2.4% drop was a convergence of geopolitical and technological shocks. The primary driver remains the ongoing conflict involving the   ...    2026, has effectively halted 20% of the world’s petroleum flow, pushing Brent crude prices toward the $110 per barrel   ...    Adding to the macro-economic gloom is the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance. Following the FOMC meeting on March 18, 2026, Chair Jerome Powell indicated that the central bank would likely only implement a single rate cut this year, a sharp pivot from the three cuts anticipated just months ago. With the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield pushing toward 4.42%,   ...    costs, means that the next phase of the market may favor value and stability over pure growth. ## What Lies Ahead: The Road to Recovery In the short term, all eyes will be on the upcoming April earnings season. Investors will be looking for guidance on how the energy crisis and the "TurboQuant" shift are impacting corporate bottom lines. If companies can demonstrate that they have the pricing power to pass on higher energy costs, the Nasdaq may find a floor. However, if margins begin to compress, the correction could deepen into a full-blown bear market (a 20% decline). Strategically, many tech firms will be forced to pivot. We expect a renewed focus on "efficiency" rather than "growth   ...    software-based efficiency gains like "TurboQuant" become the industry standard. Additionally, the shift toward domestic energy security and localized supply chains will likely become the dominant investment theme for the remainder of 2026. Potential scenarios range from a "V-shaped" recovery—if a diplomatic solution is found in the Middle East—to a prolonged "L-shaped" stagnation if oil stays above $100 and the Fed stays hawkish. Market participants should also watch for any signs of the Fed "blinking" in response to financial instability, though Powell’s recent comments suggest the central bank is prepared to tolerate some market pain to keep inflation in check.   ...    The Nasdaq’s entry into correction territory on March 26, 2026, is a sobering reminder that even the most powerful technological trends are not immune to the realities of geopolitics and macroeconomics. The 2.4% drop was a loud signal   ...    Moving forward, the market is likely to remain highly sensitive to headlines from the Middle East and the Fed’s   ...    In the

results.title: The Nasdaq Composite Is Officially in Correction Territory: 3 Things You Need to Know | The Motley Fool
results.author: Sean Williams
highlights:
The Nasdaq Composite Is Officially in Correction Territory: 3 Things You Need to Know | The Motley Fool   ...    DJI45,166.64-1.7%-793.47

NASDAQ20,948.36-2.1%-459.72   ...    META$526.14-3.9%-$21.40

MSFT$357.04-2.4%-$8.93

NVDA$167.52-2.2%-$3.72 TSLA$361.83-2.8%-$10.28   ...    By Sean Williams – Mar 26, 2026 at 8:14PM EST

## Key Points The Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite often take the stairs up and the elevator down.   ...    Since the start of 2019, record-closing highs for Wall Street's major indexes have become almost commonplace. With the   ...    ruled the roost. But this dynamic may be coming to an end. As of the closing bell on March 26, the tech-driven Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC 2.15%) is officially in correction territory(down 10.7% from its all-time closing high). The iconic Dow Jones Industrial Average (^DJI 1.73%) and benchmark S&P 500 (^GSPC 1.67%) are on its heels, with respective pullbacks of 8.4% and 7.1% from their record-closing highs. With volatility picking up on Wall Street, here are three things you should know about the Nasdaq Composite's   ...    Image source: Getty Images.

## 1. It'll likely get worse before it gets better One of the toughest realizations for investors (especially newer investors) is that Wall Street's major stock indexes   ...    The dynamic for the Nasdaq to fall at a faster pace than it rose is arguably in place. While this isn't an encompassing list, some of the biggest headwinds for Wall Street include: - Historically pricey valuations, based on the Shiller Price-to-Earnings Ratio - A significant projected jump in inflation caused by the Iran war - Ongoing tariff and trade policy uncertainty
- The potential for an artificial intelligence bubble to form and burst - Historic division within the Federal Open Market Committee Chances are that things will get worse for the Nasdaq Composite before they get better.   ...    regardless of whether the correction ends quickly or turns into a bear market. Opportunistic long-term investors can start their research in the tech software space or perhaps the financial sector if they're looking for bargains amid a historically pricey stock market. ## Read Next

results.title: Nasdaq 100 Enters Correction, Falls 11% as Big Tech Slides — TradingView News
highlights:
Nasdaq 100 Enters Correction, Falls 11% as Big Tech Slides — TradingView News 

 

Search

EN

Get started # Nasdaq 100 Enters Correction, Falls 11% as Big Tech Slides

1 min read   ...    The Nasdaq 100 QQQ has slipped into correction territory, signaling a sharp shift in market tone as the index fell 1.1% to 23,335.13 and now sits about 11% below its October peak. This marks the first correction since the tariff-driven selloff tied to Donald Trump in April 2025, and the move appears to be unfolding against a backdrop of rising geopolitical tension from the Iran war alongside growing investor hesitation toward the scale and timing of returns from artificial intelligence spending. Market participants are reacting quickly to these overlapping pressures, with sentiment turning more cautious as headline risk begins to outweigh the strong leadership tech had maintained in   ...    The selling pressure has been concentrated in the largest technology names that previously powered the rally. Microsoft Corp. MSFT and Meta Platforms Inc. META have emerged as notable drags since the index peaked on Oct. 29, falling 34% and 29% respectively, with Meta also facing additional downside linked to legal issues. The weakness is not limited to heavy spenders on AI infrastructure. Nvidia Corp. NVDA, a key beneficiary of   ...    accelerators could be. Meanwhile, software names including Workday Inc. and Atlassian Corp. have dropped more than 40%,   ...    Even so, the longer-term setup may not be fully broken. The so-called Magnificent Seven Nvidia Corp., Microsoft Corp., Apple Inc. (AAPL), Alphabet Inc. GOOG, Amazon.com Inc. AMZN, Meta Platforms Inc. and Tesla Inc. TSLA are still projected to deliver profit growth of 19% in 2026, compared with 16% for the rest of the S&P 500. At the same time, valuations have reset meaningfully, with the Nasdaq 100 now trading around 21 times estimated earnings versus 28 times at its October peak and slightly below its longer-term average. That combination could suggest that while near-term sentiment has weakened, these companies may remain positioned to benefit if confidence around AI monetization begins to

results.title: Nasdaq Slid Into Correction As War Jitters Hit Tech - Finimize
highlights:
Nasdaq Slid Into Correction As War Jitters Hit Tech - Finimize

Free Newsletter

Free Newsletter

Research News

Guides

Markets

Events

For Business   ...    # Nasdaq Slid Into Correction As War Jitters Hit Tech With oil up and Meta sliding on youth-safety rulings, investors are backing away from big tech and bracing for more   ...    Finimize Newsroom

2 mins

What's going on here? The Nasdaq Composite has slid into a correction, down 10% from its recent record, as higher oil prices and escalating conflict risk in the Middle East spooked investors. What does this mean? Friday’s selloff dragged all major US indexes lower, with the Nasdaq hit hardest and the S&P 500 heading for another weekly drop. A 4% jump in US crude revived inflation worries, which is a problem for tech because higher rates tend to hit long-duration growth valuations the most. The sector was already shaky after earlier software-led losses and fresh questions about whether AI spending is getting ahead of itself. Adding to the nerves, Meta sank after   ...    youth-safety disclosures, raising the risk of hefty fines and more lawsuits. Even if headlines cool off quickly, chart-watchers note weakening momentum, which can keep risk appetite muted. Why should I care?

For markets: When market leaders stumble, the whole index feels it. Big tech has done a lot of the heavy lifting for US stocks, so when the “Magnificent Seven” stop leading, selling can spread beyond a few names. If oil-driven inflation fears persist, investors may keep favoring cash flows today over promises tomorrow, which often pressures high-multiple software and internet stocks. That setup can also widen performance gaps between energy, defensives, and growth – making the overall market feel more fragile. Zooming out: Corrections are normal but the catalysts matter. A 10% pullback after a strong run isn’t unusual, but this one is being fueled by a tricky mix of geopolitics, oil shocks, and rising legal and regulatory risk for tech. If crude keeps reacting to conflict headlines, it could slow the disinflation trend and complicate the path for central banks. That tends to prolong volatility, especially for the growth stocks that dominated the last cycle.   ...    Theodora Lee Joseph, CFA

### This Little-Known Defense Stock Is Seeing Big Growth – And Room To Run Reda Farran, CFA

### Six Popular Inflation Hedges, And The Ones Most Likely To Work Now   ...    ### Markets Are Shaky, But Some Opportunities Are Starting To Emerge

args.thought: Synthesizing all data for QID hypothesis verification. Key findings: (1) NASDAQ-100 already in correction territory (-10-11% from Oct 2025 peak), (2) Technical breakdown confirmed - QQQ below all major SMAs, inverted cup-and-handle pattern, (3) Catalysts validated: oil at $100+ (74.6% Polymarket probability), Fed hawkish pivot (1 cut vs 3 expected), April earnings risk, (4) QID decay risk is REAL - 5Y return -83.56%, 3Y -36.92%, daily reset mechanics punish holds >5 days, (5) Big tech already down significantly (MSFT -34%, META -29% from peaks). The 3-week hold is the critical risk - QID decay could erase gains even if QQQ falls. Verdict: Thesis directionally correct but instrument choice problematic for 3-week horizon.

Final synthesis: QID hypothesis verification complete. Thesis has merit but critical flaws. PROS: (1) NASDAQ-100 already in correction (-11% from peak), (2) Technical breakdown confirmed (QQQ below all SMAs, inverted cup-and-handle), (3) Catalysts align (oil $100+, Fed hawkish, earnings risk). CONS: (1) QID decay is severe (-83% over 5Y), (2) 3-week hold amplifies volatility drag, (3) Some catalysts already priced in. Better alternatives: (1) QQQ puts for defined risk, (2) Short QQQ directly, (3) Reduce hold period to <1 week. Verdict: Directionally correct, instrument/timing suboptimal.
